questionText: Does your organization invest in research and development (R&D) of low-carbon products or services related to your sector activities?
questionKo: 귀사는 invest in research 및 development (R&D) of low-carbon products 또는 services related to your sector activities입니까?
sectorTags: Oil & Gas, Coal, Aviation, Capital goods, Transport OEMS - EPM, Chemicals, Metals & mining, Transport services, Steel, Transport OEMS, Energy utilities & power generators, Cement, Real estate, Construction
simpleHelp: Investment in R&D of new low-carbon technologies is needed to mitigate transition risk. According to the TCFD recommendations, the level of investment provides an indication of how exposed future earning capacity is to climate risks.

guidance.text:
Low-carbon product or service: CDP broadly defines a low-carbon product/service as a product or service which has comparatively lower emissions across its entire life cycle (i.e. from material acquisition through to product end-of-life) when compared to a baseline (business-as-usual) scenario or reference product of a similar function. Note that a product can only be considered low-carbon if its production and use does not prevent and/or contributes to reaching net-zero by 2050 or sooner. To define whether the product or service is low-carbon, CDP encourages the use of existing industry taxonomies and frameworks such as Climate Bonds Taxonomy , the IEA Energy Technology Perspectives (ETP) Clean Energy Technology Guide , and the EU Taxonomy for Environmentally Sustainable Economic Activities .
Research and development (R&D): Refers to the activities companies undertake to innovate and introduce new products and services. It is often the first stage in the development process. Investment in R&D is a type of operating expense associated with the research and development of a company's goods or services.
guidance.textKo: Low-carbon product 또는 service: CDP broadly defines a low-carbon product/service as a product 또는 service which has comparatively lower emissions across its entire life cycle (i.e. from material acquisition through to product end-of-life) 경우 compared to a baseline (business-as-usual) scenario 또는 reference product of a similar function. Note that a product can only be considered low-carbon 만약 its production 및 use does not prevent 및/또는 contributes to reaching net-zero by 2050 또는 sooner. To define whether the product 또는 service is low-carbon, CDP encourages the use of existing industry taxonomies 및 frameworks such as Climate Bonds Taxonomy, the IEA Energy 기술 Perspectives (ETP) Clean Energy 기술 Guide, 및 the EU Taxonomy for Environmentally Sustainable Economic Activities. Research 및 development (R&D): Refers to the activities companies undertake to innovate 및 introduce 신규 제품 및 서비스. It is often the first stage in the development 프로세스. Investment in R&D is a type of operating expense associated with the research 및 development of a company's goods 또는 services.

criteria.criteria:
ROUTE A)
Any option selected in column 'Investment in low-carbon R&D' - 2 points
A maximum of 2/2 points is available for this route.
OR
NON-DISCLOSURE ROUTE)
Column 'Investment in low-carbon R&D' NOT completed - 0/7 points
A maximum of 0/7 points is available for this route.
criteria.criteriaKo: 경로 A) 선택지가 하나라도 선택된 경우 in 열 'Investment in 저탄소 R&D' - 2 점 이 경로에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 2/2점입니다. 또는 비공개 경로) 열 'Investment in 저탄소 R&D' NOT 완성 - 0/7 점 이 경로에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 0/7점입니다.

sectorTagsKo: 석유 및 가스, 석탄, 항공, 자본재, 운송 OEM - EPM, 화학, 금속 및 광업, 운송 서비스, 철강, 운송 OEM, 에너지 유틸리티 및 발전, 시멘트, 부동산, 건설
simpleHelpKo: Investment in R&D of 신규 low-carbon technologies is needed to mitigate transition risk. According to the TCFD recommendations, the level of investment provides an indication of how exposed future earning capacity is to climate 리스크.
